properties.remark: CONSIDERABLE FLOODWATER FLOWING THROUGH WHITLOW CANYON AND INTO QUEEN CREEK, RESULTING IN THE CLOSURE OF SILVER KING RD. VIDEO OF THE FLOW WAS SHARED AS WELL AND A NEARBY DOWNSTREAM GAUGE ON QUEEN CREEK MEASURED FLOW AROUND 7 FEET HIGH.

DELAYED REPORT - VIDEO SUBMITTED ON TWITTER SHOWING CIRCLE MOUNTAIN ROAD INUNDATED BY FAST-RUNNING WASH. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR/GAUGES. DOWNSTREAM MARICOPA FLOOD GAUGE ON SKUNK CREEK PEAKED AT 1.70 FT AROUND 6 PM.
AWOS STATION KNOZ YUMA AUX.
CASANDRO WASH AT WICKENBURG REACHED ALARM STAGE, CURRENTLY 3.04 FEET.
PORTIONS OF VULTURE MINE RD CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING.
PORTIONS OF JACK BURDEN RD CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING.
SR-86 CLOSED FROM RIVERSIDE COUNTY LINE TO POE ROAD DUE TO DUST AND STRONG WINDS. CALTRANS REPORTED NEAR ZERO VISIBILITY NEAR SR-86 AND SR-78.

Approximately 9 tractor trailer rigs were blown over and/or jackknifed along I-10 near Vicksburg Rd. This occurred at two to three locations from milepost 41 to milepost 47 with both directions of traffic affected - especially westbound. Time estimated from first 911 call and radar.

Broadcast footage showed several large trees uprooted which caused property damage along Skyline Dr. Wind speeds estimated around 60 to 70 mph due to a downburst at the time.

A stream gauge at Skunk Tank Wash near the intersection of Desert Hills Drive and North 7th Ave in Anthem reported flow reaching flood stage at 7.09 feet.
Water rescue reported in the area of West Joy Ranch Road and North Pioneer Road near Anthem.
Water rescue reported near 800 E Cloud Road in Anthem.
Water rescue reported near the intersection of North New River Road and North 67th Avenue.

Spotter report of visibility down to 0.25 to 1 mile in Yuma, along I-8, between 1340-1352 MST.
***Delayed report*** Rural road between County 2nd St and County 3rd St closed due to significant flow in Gila River. Timing estimated based on radar.
Semi truck flipped and on its side.
Multiple vehicles stopped on highway 78 due to flooding.

***Delayed report*** Photo taken on 9/2 shows road erosion and evidence of considerable flow within the typically dry Gila River. A USGS gauge on the Gila River near Dome/Yuma, roughly 25 miles downstream, recorded a rapid 4 foot rise later in the evening. Timing estimated from radar.
Ogilby Road closed due to flooding.
Several power poles knocked over and nearly on the ground and in the road.
Semi truck rollover blocking both lanes of highway 86.
Incident report of flooding along highway 95, prompting a potential closure.
Report of roadway flooding.
Report of flooding on highway 95 near mile post 59.
Report of vehicles stuck on highway 78 due to flooding.
Vehicle stuck between flooded washes.
Corrects previous flash flood report from 1 W Dixieland. Vehicle swept off of road and flooding is making it difficult for responders to access the scene. A request for road closure was made.
Report of a mudslide taking out a vehicle.
Report of flash flooding on highway 177. A request to close the highway was made.
Highway 78 closed due to flash flooding, with multiple washes flooded and some vehicles trapped or stranded..
Report of significant flooding in Niland.
Report of a compromised road and failing bridge along highway 111 near Mecca Ave due to flash flooding. Highway 111 has been closed down to the county border.
Report of highway 98 being closed due to flooding and that 2 vehicles were stranded in a flooded wash, with one occupant having to climb out onto their roof.
Report of a bridge under water where Westmorland Rd transitions to Boley Rd.
***Delayed report*** 24th St closed between Ave 5 E and Z Fun Factory due to flash flooding and a mud slide. Time based on radar.
Delayed Report. Virginia Drive closed between 24th St. and 28th St. due to flooding.
***Delayed report*** Significant street flooding along Madison Ave with water encroaching on homes.

***Delayed report*** USGS gauge on Bouse Wash in Bouse eclipsed action stage around 1415MST and crested near Moderate flood stage at 2150MST. Flow did not fall back below action stage until 0730MST on 9/3.
***Delayed report*** Video on social media shows a semi-truck driving through a flooded low water crossing with estimated 6 inches of water flowing over the road. Time estimated from radar.
Corrects previous flash flood report from 4 ESE Yuma. Delayed report. 24th Street had swift flowing water over it near South Avenue 5E.
***Delayed report***Report of a swift water rescue for a single occupied vehicle near Rabbit Ln and Stagecoach Rd.
***Delayed report*** Report of numerous flooded washes along Highway 60 between mile markers 31 and 50. Time estimated from radar.
Delayed report. 2.04 inches of rain in one hour. Area washes running with the main wash in Tacna at bankfull.
***Delayed report*** Photo of elevated flow within Tyson Wash through La Paz Valley leading to an impassable low water crossing.
***Delayed report*** Kuehn St was closed due to flash flooding in Tyson Wash. Timing estimated based on USGS gauge response on Main St.
***Delayed report*** USGS gauge on Tyson wash in Quartzsite saw a rapid rise in flood waters and reached action stage at 1812MST.
***Delayed report*** Quail Trail St was closed due to flash flooding within Tyson Wash. Timing estimated based on USGS gauge response on Main St.
***Delayed report*** Quartzsite Fire and Police performed a swift water rescue of two juveniles trapped on a small island in Tyson Wash.
Delayed Report. Three downed power poles along West Miller Road about two miles west of Highway 347. Timing based on radar.

Multiple small aircraft damaged, with some even completely flipped over. There was also significant roof damage to the hangar.

Delayed Report. North Cox Road was closed between West Randolph Road and West McCartney Road due to downed power lines. There was also another report of downed power lines along the area of North Pinal Ave and West Val Vista Boulevard. Timing estimated from radar.
Home weather station with 7 ft anemometer height. Recorded southwest winds with a maximum gust of 59 mph, causing about 20 roof tiles to come off and other damage nearby.
Tree damage. Large limbs down and trees uprooted. Ceramic tiles on rooftops damaged.
Delayed report. State Route 88 closed between mileposts 229 and 242 due to flooding and rockfalls making the road impassable.
Low water crossings along Weekes Wash in Apache Junction got deep enough to initiate road closings at about 6:40 this morning.
Delayed Report. Spring Creek Wash in Roosevelt was running about 3 feet deep. Accumulated rainfall since about 930pm the previous night was 2.70 inches.
